Nephi's efforts to obtain the plates of brass show how the principles work. When the older brethren were asked to go, they murmured and received no help. Nephi was assured, "Thou shalt be favored of the Lord, because thou hast not murmured" (1 Nephi 3:6). day-labourers. The government officials, superior officers, tax-gatherers, and schoolmasters use this short rod to threaten or if necessary to beat their inferiors, whoever they may be.' The usage is very ancient. 'A blow for a slave' is the ancient maxim in Ahikar, and the proper designation of an underling is abida-'asa, 'stick-servant.'
